intent: Check whether the candidate can define Express middleware as the ordered request-response pipeline and explain what a middleware can do to req, res, and control flow.
primaryQuestion: What is middleware in Express, and how does it work?
expectedSignals: Defines middleware as a function that runs during the request-response cycle and has access to req, res, and next.
Explains that Express applications are built as an ordered stack of middleware and route handlers.
Mentions middleware can execute code, read or mutate req and res, end the response, or pass control to the next matching middleware.
Recognizes that route handlers are also part of the middleware system, not a completely separate mechanism.
Uses a concrete example such as logging, body parsing, authentication, validation, or error handling.
redFlags: Describes middleware as only a plugin package or only code that runs before routes.
Cannot explain req, res, and next.
Thinks Express automatically chooses middleware based on best fit rather than registration order.
Does not understand that middleware can either continue the chain or finish the request-response cycle.

scoringHints.description: Gives a precise definition, explains ordered pipeline execution, names the possible actions, and uses a realistic Express example.
Understands middleware as functions between request and response but misses one detail such as route handlers being middleware or mutation of req/res.
Can name middleware examples but cannot describe the request flow clearly.
Answer is too vague to prove practical Express middleware understanding.

followUps.question: Can you walk through a request that passes through a logger, express.json(), auth middleware, and a route handler?
followUps.purpose: Force the candidate to explain execution flow instead of only defining the term.

followUps.question: What are two examples of middleware that only prepare data for later handlers?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate understands pass-through middleware.

en.title: Express middleware concept
en.displayQuestion: What is middleware in Express, and how does it work?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands middleware as the ordered request-response pipeline in Express.
guidance: Define middleware as a function with access to req, res, and next.
Explain that Express executes matching middleware in the order it was registered.
Mention the four core actions: run code, modify req/res, end the response, or call next.
Use concrete examples such as logging, parsing, authentication, validation, and error handling.
commonMistakes: Saying middleware is only third-party plugins.
Forgetting that route handlers are middleware functions too.
Ignoring the effect of registration order.
Not distinguishing pass-through middleware from middleware that sends a response.

vi.title: Khái niệm middleware trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Middleware trong Express là gì và nó hoạt động như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu middleware là pipeline xử lý request-response theo thứ tự trong Express hay không.
guidance: Định nghĩa middleware là function có quyền truy cập req, res và next.
Giải thích Express chạy các middleware khớp request theo đúng thứ tự được đăng ký.
Nêu bốn việc middleware có thể làm: chạy logic, chỉnh req/res, kết thúc response, hoặc gọi next.
Dùng ví dụ cụ thể như logging, parse body, authentication, validation và error handling.
commonMistakes: Nói middleware chỉ là plugin bên thứ ba.
Quên rằng route handler cũng là middleware function.
Bỏ qua ảnh hưởng của thứ tự đăng ký.
Không phân biệt middleware pass-through với middleware gửi response.

ja.title: Express middleware の概念
ja.displayQuestion: Express の middleware とは何で、どのように動作しますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express middleware を順序付きの request-response pipeline として理解しているかを確認する。
guidance: middleware は req、res、next にアクセスできる function だと定義する。
Express は登録順に matching middleware を実行することを説明する。
code 実行、req/res 変更、response 終了、next 呼び出しという主な動作を述べる。
logging、body parsing、authentication、validation、error handling などの例を使う。
commonMistakes: middleware を third-party plugin だけだと説明する。
route handler も middleware function である点を忘れる。
登録順の影響を無視する。
pass-through middleware と response を返す middleware を区別しない。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for middleware definition, actions, and middleware categories.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Writing middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for request-response cycle and custom middleware examples.

intent: Verify whether the candidate knows the standard middleware function signature and can distinguish normal middleware from error-handling middleware.
primaryQuestion: Which parameters does an Express middleware function receive? What is the standard function signature?
expectedSignals: States the standard normal middleware signature as (req, res, next).
Explains req represents the incoming request, res represents the outgoing response, and next passes control.
Mentions that names are conventional but the parameter position matters.
Distinguishes error-handling middleware with the four-argument signature (err, req, res, next).
Notes that omitting next is acceptable only when the middleware always ends the response or intentionally does not continue.
redFlags: Uses the wrong order such as (res, req, next).
Cannot explain what next represents.
Thinks error-handling middleware has the same three-argument signature as normal middleware.
Assumes Express identifies middleware by TypeScript types or parameter names rather than runtime function shape.

scoringHints.description: Correctly explains (req, res, next), parameter responsibilities, positional meaning, and the special error middleware signature.
Knows the standard signature but under-explains error middleware or positional behavior.
Has seen req and res but cannot reliably describe next or error middleware.
Answer is inaccurate enough that the candidate may write broken middleware.

followUps.question: Why does Express require four parameters for an error-handling middleware even if you do not use next?
followUps.purpose: Check recognition of the error middleware runtime signature.

followUps.question: In (req, res, next), what object would you read headers from and what object would you call json() on?

en.title: Middleware function signature
en.displayQuestion: Which parameters does an Express middleware function receive? What is the standard function signature?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate knows normal and error-handling middleware signatures.
guidance: State the normal signature as (req, res, next).
Explain each parameter's role and that order matters.
Mention the error-handling signature (err, req, res, next).
Clarify that names are convention, while position and arity are what Express observes.
commonMistakes: Swapping req and res.
Treating next as optional in pass-through middleware.
Using a three-argument function for centralized errors.
Thinking Express relies on parameter names.

vi.title: Chữ ký hàm middleware
vi.displayQuestion: Middleware function trong Express nhận những tham số nào? Chữ ký hàm chuẩn là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có nắm signature của middleware thường và error-handling middleware hay không.
guidance: Nêu signature thường là (req, res, next).
Giải thích vai trò từng tham số và nhấn mạnh thứ tự tham số quan trọng.
Nhắc signature xử lý lỗi là (err, req, res, next).
Làm rõ tên tham số là convention, còn vị trí và số lượng tham số mới là điều Express dựa vào.
commonMistakes: Đảo req và res.
Xem next là tùy chọn trong middleware cần pass-through.
Dùng function ba tham số cho centralized error handler.
Nghĩ Express dựa vào tên tham số.

ja.title: middleware function signature
ja.displayQuestion: Express middleware function はどの引数を受け取り、標準的な signature は何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: 通常 middleware と error-handling middleware の signature を理解しているかを確認する。
guidance: 通常の signature は (req, res, next) だと述べる。
各 parameter の役割と順序の重要性を説明する。
error-handling middleware は (err, req, res, next) だと述べる。
parameter 名は convention で、Express は位置と引数数を見ていることを説明する。
commonMistakes: req と res を入れ替える。
pass-through middleware で next を不要だと考える。
centralized error handler を 3 引数 function で書く。
Express が parameter 名に依存すると考える。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Writing middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for req, res, next and custom middleware signatures.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for the error-handling middleware signature.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands next() as control transfer and can diagnose hanging requests caused by missing completion paths.
primaryQuestion: What is next() used for in Express middleware? What happens if you do not call next()?
expectedSignals: Explains next() calls the next matching middleware or route handler in the current stack.
States that middleware must either call next(), send/end the response, or intentionally delegate to an error path.
Explains that if middleware neither calls next() nor ends the response, the request remains open until the client or server times out.
Mentions that calling next() after sending a final response can cause duplicate response or headers-already-sent bugs.
Recognizes special forms such as next('route'), next('router'), and next(err) at a high level.
redFlags: Says next() sends the response to the client.
Says Express automatically continues when the middleware function returns.
Calls next() unconditionally after res.json() without explaining why that can be unsafe.
Cannot explain why a request might hang.

scoringHints.description: Explains control transfer, completion paths, hanging requests, duplicate-send risks, and special next forms accurately.
Understands next() and hanging requests but misses special forms or duplicate response hazards.
Knows to call next() but cannot reason about when not to call it.
Answer shows a broken mental model of Express control flow.

followUps.question: What would happen if an auth middleware finds no token, calls res.status(401).json(...), and then still calls next()?
followUps.purpose: Check duplicate response and authorization bypass awareness.

followUps.question: When would next('route') be useful?
followUps.purpose: Probe knowledge of route stack skipping without requiring deep internals.

en.title: next() and control flow
en.displayQuestion: What is next() used for in Express middleware? What happens if you do not call next()?
en.displayIntent: Check request completion paths and middleware flow control.
guidance: Explain next() as passing control to the next matching middleware.
State that each path must either continue, end the response, or forward an error.
Mention hanging requests when no completion path is reached.
Warn about calling next() after a final response.
commonMistakes: Thinking next() sends a response.
Relying on function return to continue the chain.
Calling next() after res.send() in every case.
Ignoring timeout symptoms from missing next().

vi.title: next() và luồng điều khiển
vi.displayQuestion: next() dùng để làm gì? Điều gì xảy ra nếu không gọi next()?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu completion path của request và flow control trong middleware hay không.
guidance: Giải thích next() dùng để chuyển quyền xử lý cho middleware khớp tiếp theo.
Nêu rõ mỗi nhánh xử lý phải tiếp tục, kết thúc response, hoặc forward lỗi.
Nhắc request sẽ bị treo nếu không có completion path.
Cảnh báo lỗi khi gọi next() sau khi đã gửi response cuối cùng.
commonMistakes: Nghĩ next() gửi response cho client.
Tin rằng return khỏi function sẽ tự động chạy middleware tiếp theo.
Luôn gọi next() sau res.send().
Bỏ qua triệu chứng timeout do quên next().

ja.title: next() と control flow
ja.displayQuestion: Express middleware の next() は何に使い、呼ばないと何が起きますか。
ja.displayIntent: request の完了経路と middleware flow control を理解しているかを確認する。
guidance: next() は次の matching middleware に制御を渡すものだと説明する。
各 branch は継続、response 終了、error forwarding のいずれかを行うべきだと述べる。
completion path が無いと request が hanging することを説明する。
final response 後に next() を呼ぶ危険を説明する。
commonMistakes: next() が response を送ると考える。
function return だけで chain が続くと考える。
常に res.send() の後で next() を呼ぶ。
next() 忘れによる timeout を見落とす。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Writing middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for next() behavior, hanging requests, and Express 5 promise rejection forwarding.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for next('route') and next('router') behavior.

intent: Check whether the candidate can classify Express middleware types and give practical examples for each category.
primaryQuestion: What types of middleware are there in Express? Include application-level, router-level, built-in, third-party, and error-handling middleware.
expectedSignals: Names application-level middleware registered on app with app.use() or app.METHOD().
Names router-level middleware registered on an express.Router instance with router.use() or router.METHOD().
Names built-in middleware such as express.json(), express.urlencoded(), and express.static().
Names third-party middleware such as cors, morgan, helmet, cookie-parser, compression, or session middleware.
Names error-handling middleware and gives the (err, req, res, next) signature.
Explains that the categories describe where middleware comes from or how it is registered, not mutually exclusive business responsibilities.
redFlags: Can list package names but cannot explain application-level versus router-level.
Omits error-handling middleware or gives the wrong signature.
Claims built-in middleware includes every middleware Express apps commonly use.
Confuses middleware type with HTTP method type.

scoringHints.description: Covers all five requested categories, examples, registration API, and the special error-handler signature.
Lists most categories and examples but under-explains one distinction.
Only knows third-party middleware names without classification.
Cannot categorize middleware in a way that helps structure an Express app.

followUps.question: Where would you put express.json(), cors(), morgan(), and a final error handler in a typical app?
Why is express.Router() useful for feature-specific middleware?

en.title: Types of Express middleware
en.displayQuestion: What types of middleware are there in Express? Include application-level, router-level, built-in, third-party, and error-handling middleware.
en.displayIntent: Check middleware classification and practical examples.
guidance: Name each category and the registration API where relevant.
Give at least one concrete example for built-in and third-party middleware.
Call out the four-argument error middleware signature.
Explain router-level middleware as scoped to a Router instance.
commonMistakes: Listing only package names.
Forgetting error-handling middleware.
Confusing app-level and router-level scope.
Treating middleware categories as HTTP methods.

vi.title: Các loại middleware trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Các loại middleware trong Express là gì? Bao gồm application-level, router-level, built-in, third-party và error-handling.
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có phân loại được middleware và đưa ví dụ thực tế hay không.
guidance: Nêu từng loại và API đăng ký tương ứng nếu có.
Đưa ít nhất một ví dụ cụ thể cho built-in và third-party middleware.
Nhấn mạnh error middleware có bốn tham số.
Giải thích router-level middleware được scope theo Router instance.
commonMistakes: Chỉ liệt kê tên package.
Quên error-handling middleware.
Nhầm scope app-level và router-level.
Xem loại middleware như HTTP method.

ja.title: Express middleware の種類
ja.displayQuestion: Express にはどのような middleware の種類がありますか。application-level、router-level、built-in、third-party、error-handling を含めて説明してください。
ja.displayIntent: middleware の分類と実務的な例を理解しているかを確認する。
guidance: 各 category と該当する登録 API を述べる。
built-in と third-party middleware の具体例を少なくとも 1 つずつ挙げる。
error middleware は 4 引数 signature であることを明確にする。
router-level middleware は Router instance に scoped されると説明する。
commonMistakes: package 名だけを列挙する。
error-handling middleware を忘れる。
app-level と router-level の scope を混同する。
middleware category を HTTP method として扱う。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for official middleware categories and examples.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: 4.x API
sourceReferences.note: Used for built-in middleware API references.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can register middleware with app.use(), understand optional mount paths, and avoid accidentally applying middleware too broadly.
primaryQuestion: How is app.use() used to register middleware in Express?
expectedSignals: Explains app.use(middleware) registers middleware for all HTTP methods on matching requests.
Explains app.use('/prefix', middleware) mounts middleware only for paths under that prefix.
Mentions app.use() can receive multiple middleware functions, arrays of middleware, or an express.Router instance.
Distinguishes app.use() from app.get(), app.post(), or other method-specific route handlers.
Mentions registration order still controls when the middleware runs.
redFlags: Thinks app.use() only registers global middleware and cannot be mounted on a path.
Assumes app.use('/api', mw) only runs for GET requests.
Mounts authentication globally without considering public endpoints.
Does not realize a Router can be mounted with app.use().

scoringHints.description: Explains global and path-mounted app.use(), multi-middleware registration, Router mounting, method behavior, and order.
Understands basic app.use() but misses Router mounting or method-agnostic behavior.
Can copy app.use(express.json()) but cannot reason about mount paths or scope.
Answer would likely lead to incorrectly scoped middleware.

followUps.question: What is the difference between app.use('/admin', auth) and app.get('/admin', auth, handler)?
How would you mount all user routes under /api/users using a Router?

en.title: Registering middleware with app.use
en.displayQuestion: How is app.use() used to register middleware in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check global, path-mounted, and Router-based middleware registration.
guidance: Explain app.use(mw) and app.use('/prefix', mw).
Mention that app.use() is method-agnostic for matching paths.
Describe passing multiple middleware functions or a Router.
Connect app.use() behavior to registration order.
commonMistakes: Thinking app.use() is only global.
Forgetting that it applies across HTTP methods.
Mounting middleware too broadly.
Not knowing Router instances can be mounted.

vi.title: Đăng ký middleware bằng app.use
vi.displayQuestion: app.use() được dùng để đăng ký middleware như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách đăng ký middleware global, theo path prefix và qua Router.
guidance: Giải thích app.use(mw) và app.use('/prefix', mw).
Nhắc rằng app.use() không giới hạn theo HTTP method khi path khớp.
Mô tả cách truyền nhiều middleware function hoặc Router.
Liên hệ app.use() với thứ tự đăng ký.
commonMistakes: Nghĩ app.use() chỉ dùng cho global middleware.
Quên rằng nó áp dụng cho nhiều HTTP method.
Mount middleware quá rộng.
Không biết có thể mount Router instance.

ja.title: app.use による middleware 登録
ja.displayQuestion: Express で app.use() は middleware 登録にどのように使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: global、path-mounted、Router-based middleware registration を理解しているかを確認する。
guidance: app.use(mw) と app.use('/prefix', mw) を説明する。
app.use() は matching path に対して method-agnostic であることを述べる。
複数 middleware function や Router を渡せることを説明する。
app.use() の挙動を登録順と結びつける。
commonMistakes: app.use() は global 専用だと考える。
HTTP method をまたいで適用される点を忘れる。
middleware を広すぎる scope に mount する。
Router instance を mount できることを知らない。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for app.use(), mount paths, sub-stacks, and Router mounting.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: 4.x API app.use
sourceReferences.note: Used for application-level middleware mounting behavior.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can reason about middleware order and diagnose bugs caused by registering middleware before or after the routes that need it.
primaryQuestion: Does the order of registering middleware matter in Express? Why?
expectedSignals: States that order matters because Express executes matching middleware in the order it is registered.
Explains parsers such as express.json() must run before routes that read req.body.
Explains auth and authorization middleware must run before protected handlers.
Explains a route that sends a response can prevent later normal middleware from running for that request.
Mentions error-handling middleware is usually registered after routes so it can catch forwarded errors.
Recognizes that broad middleware registered too early can affect public routes unintentionally.
redFlags: Claims Express reorders middleware automatically.
Registers error middleware before routes and expects it to catch later route errors.
Cannot explain why req.body is undefined.
Ignores the fact that response-ending middleware can stop the rest of the normal chain.

scoringHints.description: Explains ordered execution with parser, auth, route-ending, static, and error-handler placement examples.
Understands registration order but misses one placement pattern such as error handlers.
Knows order matters but cannot diagnose a concrete ordering bug.
Answer does not show reliable Express pipeline reasoning.

followUps.question: Why would req.body be undefined if express.json() is below the route definition?
If a route sends res.send('ok'), will a logger registered after that route still run for the same request?

en.title: Middleware registration order
en.displayQuestion: Does the order of registering middleware matter in Express? Why?
en.displayIntent: Check execution-order reasoning and common ordering bugs.
guidance: State clearly that matching middleware runs in registration order.
Use examples: body parser before routes, auth before protected routes, error handler after routes.
Explain that middleware sending a response can stop later normal middleware.
Mention route scope and broad middleware side effects.
commonMistakes: Assuming Express sorts middleware by type.
Registering parsers after the routes that need req.body.
Putting error handlers too early.
Forgetting that a completed response changes downstream behavior.

vi.title: Thứ tự đăng ký middleware
vi.displayQuestion: Thứ tự đăng ký middleware có quan trọng không? Tại sao?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng reasoning về thứ tự chạy và các lỗi ordering thường gặp.
guidance: Nói rõ middleware khớp request sẽ chạy theo thứ tự được đăng ký.
Dùng ví dụ: body parser trước route, auth trước route cần bảo vệ, error handler sau route.
Giải thích middleware gửi response có thể chặn middleware thường phía sau.
Nhắc tới scope route và tác dụng phụ khi middleware quá rộng.
commonMistakes: Cho rằng Express tự sắp xếp middleware theo loại.
Đăng ký parser sau route cần req.body.
Đặt error handler quá sớm.
Quên rằng response đã hoàn tất sẽ ảnh hưởng middleware phía sau.

ja.title: middleware 登録順
ja.displayQuestion: Express では middleware の登録順は重要ですか。なぜですか。
ja.displayIntent: execution order の reasoning と典型的な ordering bug を確認する。
guidance: matching middleware は登録順に実行されると明確に述べる。
body parser は route の前、auth は protected route の前、error handler は route の後という例を使う。
response を送る middleware が後続の通常 middleware を止め得ることを説明する。
route scope と広すぎる middleware の副作用に触れる。
commonMistakes: Express が middleware を種類別に自動ソートすると考える。
req.body を必要とする route の後に parser を登録する。
error handler を早すぎる位置に置く。
完了した response が後続挙動に影響する点を忘れる。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Writing middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for middleware loading order and examples.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for route stacks, response-ending handlers, and error middleware placement.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands Express built-in body parsing middleware, content types, order, limits, and untrusted input boundaries.
primaryQuestion: What are express.json() and express.urlencoded() used for?
expectedSignals: Explains express.json() parses incoming requests with JSON payloads and populates req.body.
Explains express.urlencoded() parses application/x-www-form-urlencoded payloads such as HTML form submissions and populates req.body.
Mentions they only parse requests whose Content-Type matches the configured parser.
Mentions they must be registered before routes that need req.body.
Mentions useful options such as limit and extended for urlencoded payloads.
States parsed req.body is still user-controlled and must be validated before use.
redFlags: Thinks express.json() parses query strings or path parameters.
Assumes parsed body data is safe and correctly typed.
Registers parsers after routes and expects req.body to be available.
Uses urlencoded for all payloads without considering Content-Type.

scoringHints.description: Explains parser purpose, Content-Type matching, req.body population, order, options, and validation/security boundaries.
Knows JSON and form parsing but misses limits, Content-Type, or validation nuance.
Can add express.json() but cannot explain when it runs or what risks remain.
Answer confuses body parsing with unrelated request data sources.

followUps.question: What body size limit would you set for a login endpoint, and why?
followUps.purpose: Probe operational and security thinking around parser options.

followUps.question: Can req.body.name.trim() throw after express.json() has parsed the request? Why?
followUps.purpose: Check untrusted input and runtime type awareness.

en.title: express.json and express.urlencoded
en.displayQuestion: What are express.json() and express.urlencoded() used for?
en.displayIntent: Check safe body parser usage and request body boundaries.
guidance: Map express.json() to JSON request bodies and express.urlencoded() to form-encoded bodies.
Mention req.body, Content-Type matching, and middleware order.
Discuss parser options such as limit and urlencoded extended.
Emphasize validation because req.body remains untrusted.
commonMistakes: Using body parsers for query strings.
Trusting parsed values without validation.
Registering parsers after routes.
Ignoring payload size limits.

vi.title: express.json và express.urlencoded
vi.displayQuestion: express.json() và express.urlencoded() dùng để làm gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách dùng body parser an toàn và ranh giới dữ liệu request body.
guidance: Map express.json() với JSON request body và express.urlencoded() với form-encoded body.
Nhắc req.body, Content-Type matching và thứ tự middleware.
Nói về option như limit và extended của urlencoded.
Nhấn mạnh vẫn phải validate vì req.body là dữ liệu không đáng tin.
commonMistakes: Dùng body parser để đọc query string.
Tin dữ liệu đã parse mà không validate.
Đăng ký parser sau route.
Bỏ qua giới hạn kích thước payload.

ja.title: express.json と express.urlencoded
ja.displayQuestion: express.json() と express.urlencoded() は何に使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: 安全な body parser の使い方と request body の境界を確認する。
guidance: express.json() は JSON request body、express.urlencoded() は form-encoded body に対応すると説明する。
req.body、Content-Type matching、middleware order に触れる。
limit や urlencoded の extended などの option を説明する。
req.body は信頼できないため validation が必要だと強調する。
commonMistakes: body parser で query string を読む。
parse 済みの値を validation せず信頼する。
route の後に parser を登録する。
payload size limit を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: 4.x API express.json
sourceReferences.note: Used for JSON parser behavior, options, and untrusted req.body warning.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: 4.x API express.urlencoded
sourceReferences.note: Used for URL-encoded parser behavior and options.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can design and implement custom Express middleware with correct control flow, scoping, error forwarding, and configurability.
primaryQuestion: How do you write a custom middleware in Express?
expectedSignals: Writes or describes a function with the signature (req, res, next).
Performs a focused concern such as logging, assigning request IDs, checking authentication, validating input, or timing requests.
Calls next() when the request should continue.
Ends the response intentionally for rejection paths such as invalid auth or bad input.
For async work, forwards failures with next(err) in Express 4 or relies on Express 5 promise forwarding where appropriate.
Mentions configurable middleware as a factory that accepts options and returns (req, res, next).
redFlags: Forgets next() in success paths.
Sends a response and then continues to the protected handler.
Wraps unrelated business logic into global middleware.
Catches async errors and suppresses them.

scoringHints.description: Shows correct middleware shape, completion paths, async error forwarding, route scoping, and optional factory configuration.
Can write a working custom middleware but misses configurability or async error nuance.
Understands the rough shape but likely leaves hanging or duplicate-response paths.
Cannot describe a safe custom middleware implementation.

followUps.question: Write a requestId middleware that sets req.requestId and then lets the route continue.
followUps.purpose: Force a concrete pass-through middleware example.

followUps.question: When would you make middleware configurable with a factory instead of hardcoding values inside it?

en.title: Custom Express middleware
en.displayQuestion: How do you write a custom middleware in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check implementation skill for custom middleware with correct control flow.
guidance: Start from the (req, res, next) function shape.
Choose one clear responsibility for the middleware.
Explain success, rejection, and error paths.
Mention configurable middleware factories for reusable behavior.
commonMistakes: Forgetting next() on success.
Sending a response and then continuing.
Mixing domain business logic into global middleware.
Swallowing async errors.

vi.title: Custom middleware trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để viết một middleware tùy chỉnh trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng implement custom middleware với control flow đúng.
guidance: Bắt đầu từ function shape (req, res, next).
Chọn một trách nhiệm rõ ràng cho middleware.
Giải thích nhánh thành công, nhánh từ chối và nhánh lỗi.
Nhắc tới middleware factory có options để tái sử dụng.
commonMistakes: Quên next() ở nhánh thành công.
Gửi response rồi vẫn cho request đi tiếp.
Nhét business logic domain vào global middleware.
Nuốt lỗi async.

ja.title: Custom Express middleware
ja.displayQuestion: Express で custom middleware はどのように書きますか。
ja.displayIntent: 正しい control flow を持つ custom middleware の実装力を確認する。
guidance: (req, res, next) の function shape から始める。
middleware に明確な 1 つの責務を持たせる。
success、rejection、error path を説明する。
再利用のための options 付き middleware factory に触れる。
commonMistakes: success path で next() を忘れる。
response を送った後も処理を続ける。
domain business logic を global middleware に混ぜる。
async error を握りつぶす。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Writing middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for custom middleware examples and configurable middleware factories.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for async and next(err) error forwarding behavior.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands which middleware paths terminate the request-response cycle and which are expected to pass control onward.
primaryQuestion: Which middleware can end the request-response cycle? Which middleware normally does not?
expectedSignals: States any middleware can end the cycle if it sends or ends a response with res.send(), res.json(), res.end(), res.redirect(), res.sendStatus(), or similar.
Recognizes route handlers often terminate the cycle by returning the final response.
Explains auth, validation, rate-limit, static-file, and error middleware may terminate the cycle on rejection or when they produce a response.
Explains pass-through middleware such as logging, request IDs, body parsing, cookie parsing, CORS header setup, and metrics usually call next() after preparing data or headers.
Mentions that some middleware has conditional behavior, such as CORS handling preflight or auth ending only when credentials are invalid.
Warns against sending a response and then calling next() into normal handlers unless deliberately delegating to an error handler after headers logic.
redFlags: Claims only route handlers can send responses.
Claims all middleware must call next() no matter what.
Cannot distinguish successful pass-through from rejection paths.
Calls next() after every res.json() without understanding duplicate responses.

scoringHints.description: Explains termination as a control-flow choice, gives terminating and pass-through examples, and handles conditional middleware behavior.
Understands send-versus-next but misses conditional middleware examples.
Can list examples but cannot reason about completion paths.
Answer would cause hanging or duplicate-response bugs.

followUps.question: Is auth middleware terminating or non-terminating? Explain both success and failure paths.
Why is calling next() after res.sendStatus(401) usually a bug?

en.title: Ending the request-response cycle
en.displayQuestion: Which middleware can end the request-response cycle? Which middleware normally does not?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands terminating versus pass-through middleware behavior.
guidance: Define termination as sending, ending, redirecting, or otherwise completing the response.
Give examples of middleware that commonly terminates: route handlers, auth failures, validation failures, static files, error handlers.
Give examples of pass-through middleware: logging, parsers, request IDs, CORS header setup, metrics.
Explain conditional middleware behavior and duplicate-response risk.
commonMistakes: Saying only routes can end responses.
Saying every middleware must always call next().
Ignoring success versus failure paths.
Continuing into downstream handlers after an error response.

vi.title: Kết thúc request-response cycle
vi.displayQuestion: Middleware nào có thể kết thúc request-response cycle? Middleware nào thì thường không?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu middleware kết thúc response và middleware pass-through hay không.
guidance: Định nghĩa kết thúc cycle là gửi, end, redirect hoặc hoàn tất response.
Đưa ví dụ thường kết thúc: route handler, auth fail, validation fail, static files, error handler.
Đưa ví dụ pass-through: logging, parser, request ID, CORS header setup, metrics.
Giải thích middleware có thể conditional và rủi ro duplicate response.
commonMistakes: Nói chỉ route mới được kết thúc response.
Nói mọi middleware luôn phải gọi next().
Bỏ qua nhánh thành công và nhánh thất bại.
Đã trả error response nhưng vẫn cho handler phía sau chạy.

ja.title: request-response cycle の終了
ja.displayQuestion: どの middleware が request-response cycle を終了でき、どの middleware は通常終了しませんか。
ja.displayIntent: terminating middleware と pass-through middleware の挙動を理解しているかを確認する。
guidance: termination は response を送る、end する、redirect する、完了させることだと定義する。
route handler、auth failure、validation failure、static files、error handler などの例を挙げる。
logging、parser、request ID、CORS header setup、metrics などの pass-through 例を挙げる。
conditional middleware behavior と duplicate response risk を説明する。
commonMistakes: route だけが response を終了できると言う。
すべての middleware が必ず next() を呼ぶべきだと言う。
success path と failure path を区別しない。
error response 後も downstream handler を実行する。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for ending the request-response cycle versus calling next.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: 4.x API Response
sourceReferences.note: Used for response methods such as send, json, end, redirect, and sendStatus.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands the browser same-origin policy, what cors middleware actually changes, and why CORS is not API authentication.
primaryQuestion: What problem does the cors middleware solve in Express?
expectedSignals: Explains CORS as a browser mechanism related to the same-origin policy.
Explains cors middleware sets response headers such as Access-Control-Allow-Origin so browser JavaScript may read permitted cross-origin responses.
Mentions preflight OPTIONS requests for non-simple methods, custom headers, or credentialed scenarios.
Explains common configuration: origin allowlist, methods, allowedHeaders, exposedHeaders, credentials, and maxAge.
Warns that CORS is enforced by browsers and does not protect APIs from server-to-server, curl, Postman, or malicious direct requests.
Avoids wildcard origins with credentialed requests and separates CORS policy from authentication and authorization.
redFlags: Treats CORS as a complete security layer for APIs.
Sets origin '*' with credentials true in production.
Cannot explain preflight OPTIONS requests.
Thinks CORS blocks all non-browser clients.

scoringHints.description: Explains browser enforcement, response headers, preflight, credentialed requests, allowlists, and the difference from authentication.
Understands cross-origin browser calls but misses one configuration or security nuance.
Knows to install cors but cannot explain what headers or browser behavior are involved.
Answer shows dangerous CORS or security misconceptions.

followUps.question: Why does a request with an Authorization header often trigger a preflight OPTIONS request?
Why is CORS not a substitute for authentication?
followUps.purpose: Separate browser read permissions from API access control.

en.title: CORS middleware purpose
en.displayQuestion: What problem does the cors middleware solve in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check browser CORS mechanics and safe policy design.
guidance: Explain CORS through browser same-origin policy.
Describe the response headers set by the cors middleware.
Mention preflight and credentialed request constraints.
Clarify that CORS is not authentication and not enforced by non-browser clients.
commonMistakes: Using CORS as the only API security control.
Combining wildcard origin with credentials.
Ignoring preflight requests.
Assuming CORS blocks curl or server-to-server clients.

vi.title: Mục đích của cors middleware
vi.displayQuestion: cors middleware dùng để giải quyết vấn đề gì trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cơ chế CORS của browser và cách thiết kế policy an toàn.
guidance: Giải thích CORS thông qua same-origin policy của browser.
Mô tả các response header mà cors middleware set.
Nhắc tới preflight và ràng buộc khi request có credentials.
Làm rõ CORS không phải authentication và không được enforce bởi non-browser client.
commonMistakes: Xem CORS là lớp bảo mật duy nhất của API.
Kết hợp wildcard origin với credentials.
Bỏ qua preflight request.
Nghĩ CORS chặn curl hoặc server-to-server client.

ja.title: CORS middleware の目的
ja.displayQuestion: Express の cors middleware はどの問題を解決しますか。
ja.displayIntent: browser CORS mechanics と安全な policy design を確認する。
guidance: browser の same-origin policy を通して CORS を説明する。
cors middleware が設定する response header を説明する。
preflight と credentialed request の制約に触れる。
CORS は authentication ではなく、non-browser client には強制されないと明確にする。
commonMistakes: CORS を API の唯一の security control として使う。
wildcard origin と credentials を組み合わせる。
preflight request を無視する。
CORS が curl や server-to-server client をブロックすると考える。

sourceReferences.label: expressjs/cors README
sourceReferences.note: Used for CORS middleware purpose, options, and common misconceptions.

sourceReferences.label: Express middleware resources
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express-recognized third-party middleware context.

intent: Verify whether the candidate can apply middleware only to selected routes, prefixes, routers, or HTTP methods instead of the entire application.
primaryQuestion: How do you apply middleware only to certain routes instead of the whole Express app?
expectedSignals: Uses route-level middleware such as app.get('/admin', auth, handler) or router.post('/orders', validate, handler).
Uses path-mounted middleware such as app.use('/admin', auth) for a prefix.
Uses express.Router() to group feature routes and attach router.use(auth) only inside that group.
Mentions arrays or multiple middleware functions can be composed for a route.
Distinguishes route-specific middleware from global app.use(middleware).
Considers public endpoints such as login, health checks, and webhooks that may need different middleware.
redFlags: Registers all middleware globally because it is simpler.
Cannot protect only POST /orders without affecting GET /orders.
Applies auth to login or health routes by accident.
Does not know route handlers can accept middleware before the final handler.

scoringHints.description: Explains route-level, prefix-mounted, Router-level, method-specific, and array composition patterns with examples.
Knows route-level middleware but misses router grouping or method specificity.
Understands middleware globally but cannot scope it safely.
Answer would over-apply middleware and break public or method-specific endpoints.

followUps.question: How would you protect only /admin routes while leaving /login and /health public?
How would you apply validation only to POST /users but not GET /users?

en.title: Route-scoped middleware
en.displayQuestion: How do you apply middleware only to certain routes instead of the whole Express app?
en.displayIntent: Check route-level, prefix-level, and Router-level middleware scoping.
guidance: Use examples with app.get or router.post receiving middleware before the handler.
Explain app.use('/prefix', middleware) for path-prefix scoping.
Explain Router grouping with router.use().
Mention public routes and method-specific behavior.
commonMistakes: Making every middleware global.
Protecting login or health routes by accident.
Ignoring HTTP method-specific scoping.
Duplicating middleware instead of grouping routes.

vi.title: Middleware theo scope route
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để áp dụng middleware chỉ cho một số route nhất định thay vì toàn bộ app?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách scope middleware ở route-level, prefix-level và Router-level.
guidance: Dùng ví dụ app.get hoặc router.post có middleware trước handler.
Giải thích app.use('/prefix', middleware) để scope theo path prefix.
Giải thích gom route bằng Router và router.use().
Nhắc route public và behavior theo HTTP method.
commonMistakes: Biến mọi middleware thành global.
Vô tình bảo vệ login hoặc health route.
Bỏ qua scoping theo HTTP method.
Lặp middleware thay vì gom route hợp lý.

ja.title: route-scoped middleware
ja.displayQuestion: Express app 全体ではなく一部の route だけに middleware を適用するにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: route-level、prefix-level、Router-level の middleware scoping を確認する。
guidance: handler の前に middleware を渡す app.get や router.post の例を使う。
path prefix scope として app.use('/prefix', middleware) を説明する。
Router grouping と router.use() を説明する。
public route と HTTP method-specific behavior に触れる。
commonMistakes: すべての middleware を global にする。
login や health route を誤って保護する。
HTTP method-specific scoping を無視する。
route grouping せず middleware を重複させる。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for path-mounted middleware, route stacks, next('route'), and Router-level examples.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Routing
sourceReferences.note: Used for route handlers and method-specific route definitions.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can compare application-level and router-level middleware and choose router-level middleware for modular route groups.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between application-level middleware and router-level middleware?
expectedSignals: Explains application-level middleware is bound to the app instance using app.use() or app.METHOD().
Explains router-level middleware is bound to an express.Router instance using router.use() or router.METHOD().
States they work similarly in control flow, but differ in scope and modular organization.
Mentions router-level middleware runs only when the router is mounted and the request matches the router path.
Uses examples such as app-level logging or body parsing versus router-level admin auth or users validation.
Mentions routers help split feature modules and keep route-specific concerns local.
redFlags: Claims router-level middleware runs before all app-level middleware.
Cannot explain where an express.Router is mounted.
Uses app-level middleware for every feature-specific concern.
Thinks router-level middleware is a different syntax with different next semantics.

scoringHints.description: Compares binding target, scope, mount path, execution similarity, and modular design trade-offs with examples.
Understands app versus router scope but misses mount path or organization benefits.
Can use app.use but has weak router mental model.
Answer cannot guide modular Express route design.

followUps.question: If router.use(auth) is inside adminRouter and app.use('/admin', adminRouter), which requests does auth affect?
followUps.purpose: Verify mount-path and router scope reasoning.

followUps.question: Why might you keep admin authorization middleware in the admin router instead of app.use(auth) globally?
followUps.purpose: Check modularity and accidental scope trade-offs.

en.title: Application-level versus router-level middleware
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between application-level middleware and router-level middleware?
en.displayIntent: Check scope and modularity understanding for Express middleware.
guidance: Compare middleware bound to app with middleware bound to express.Router.
Explain that control flow is similar but scope and organization differ.
Mention router mounting and route prefix effects.
Use examples where app-level and router-level placement make sense.
commonMistakes: Thinking router middleware has different next semantics.
Ignoring the router mount path.
Putting every concern at app level.
Assuming router middleware always runs first.

vi.title: Application-level và router-level middleware
vi.displayQuestion: Sự khác biệt giữa application-level middleware và router-level middleware là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về scope và modularity của middleware trong Express.
guidance: So sánh middleware gắn vào app với middleware gắn vào express.Router.
Giải thích control flow tương tự nhưng khác scope và cách tổ chức.
Nhắc tới mount router và ảnh hưởng của route prefix.
Dùng ví dụ vị trí app-level và router-level hợp lý.
commonMistakes: Nghĩ router middleware có next semantics khác.
Bỏ qua mount path của router.
Đặt mọi concern ở app level.
Cho rằng router middleware luôn chạy trước.

ja.title: application-level と router-level middleware
ja.displayQuestion: application-level middleware と router-level middleware の違いは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: Express middleware の scope と modularity の理解を確認する。
guidance: app に bound された middleware と express.Router に bound された middleware を比較する。
control flow は似ているが scope と organization が違うと説明する。
router mounting と route prefix の影響に触れる。
app-level と router-level が適切な例を使う。
commonMistakes: router middleware は next semantics が違うと考える。
router mount path を無視する。
すべての concern を app level に置く。
router middleware が常に先に実行されると考える。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for application-level and router-level middleware comparison.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: 4.x API express.Router
sourceReferences.note: Used for Router construction and mounting context.

intent: Check whether the candidate knows morgan as HTTP request logging middleware and understands where it fits relative to application logs, privacy, and production observability.
primaryQuestion: What is morgan and what is it used for in Express?
expectedSignals: Defines morgan as HTTP request logger middleware for Node.js and Express.
Explains it logs request/response information such as method, URL, status code, response time, and content length depending on format.
Mentions common formats such as dev, combined, common, short, or custom tokens.
Explains it is useful for local debugging, access logs, latency visibility, and operational troubleshooting.
Mentions production use should route logs to the app logger or a stream and avoid logging sensitive data.
Distinguishes request access logs from domain/business/audit logs.
redFlags: Thinks morgan is an error handler or security middleware.
Logs secrets, Authorization headers, cookies, or full request bodies by default without concern.
Uses console-only logs in production without any retention or aggregation strategy.
Confuses morgan with body parsing or CORS.

scoringHints.description: Explains morgan's HTTP access-log role, formats/tokens, production stream integration, privacy boundaries, and relation to observability.
Knows morgan logs requests but misses production logging or privacy nuance.
Recognizes the package name but cannot explain what gets logged or why.
Answer confuses morgan with unrelated middleware.

followUps.question: Where would you send morgan logs in production instead of only printing to the console?
Why should you be careful about logging headers or request bodies?

en.title: Morgan request logging
en.displayQuestion: What is morgan and what is it used for in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check HTTP access logging knowledge and production logging judgment.
guidance: Define morgan as HTTP request logger middleware.
Mention common logged fields and formats.
Explain local debugging and production access-log use cases.
Discuss log streams, aggregation, and sensitive data boundaries.
commonMistakes: Treating morgan as an error handler.
Logging secrets or full bodies casually.
Relying only on console logs in production.
Confusing request logs with business audit logs.

vi.title: Morgan request logging
vi.displayQuestion: morgan là gì và được dùng để làm gì trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về HTTP access log và judgment khi logging production.
guidance: Định nghĩa morgan là HTTP request logger middleware.
Nhắc các field và format thường được log.
Giải thích use case debug local và access log production.
Nói về log stream, aggregation và ranh giới dữ liệu nhạy cảm.
commonMistakes: Xem morgan là error handler.
Log secret hoặc full body một cách tùy tiện.
Chỉ dựa vào console log ở production.
Nhầm request log với business audit log.

ja.title: Morgan request logging
ja.displayQuestion: morgan とは何で、Express では何に使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: HTTP access logging と production logging の判断を確認する。
guidance: morgan は HTTP request logger middleware だと定義する。
よく log される field と format に触れる。
local debugging と production access log の use case を説明する。
log stream、aggregation、sensitive data boundary を説明する。
commonMistakes: morgan を error handler として扱う。
secret や full body を不用意に log する。
production で console log だけに依存する。
request log と business audit log を混同する。

sourceReferences.label: expressjs/morgan README
sourceReferences.note: Used for morgan purpose, predefined formats, tokens, and stream option.

sourceReferences.label: Express performance best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for production logging and performance context.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands Helmet as security-header middleware and can explain both its value and its limits.
primaryQuestion: What is helmet and why should you use it in Express?
expectedSignals: Defines Helmet as middleware that helps secure Express apps by setting HTTP response headers.
Names example protections such as Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, cross-origin policies, frame protection, or HSTS depending on configuration.
Explains it reduces classes of browser-side risk such as clickjacking, MIME sniffing, overly permissive referrers, and some XSS impact when CSP is configured well.
Mentions Helmet is not a complete security solution and must be paired with authentication, authorization, validation, rate limiting, TLS, safe cookies, and dependency hygiene.
Recognizes some headers, especially CSP and HSTS, require context-aware configuration to avoid breaking frontend assets or local development.
Mentions placement early in the middleware stack for normal responses.
redFlags: Says installing Helmet makes the application secure by itself.
Cannot name any header or browser risk category Helmet affects.
Enables strict CSP blindly without considering scripts, styles, CDNs, or API-only behavior.
Ignores TLS and cookie security while relying only on headers.

scoringHints.description: Explains security-header purpose, concrete headers and risks, configuration trade-offs, placement, and defense-in-depth limits.
Knows Helmet sets security headers but misses configuration or defense-in-depth nuance.
Recognizes Helmet by name but cannot explain what risk it reduces.
Answer shows a misleading or unsafe security model.

followUps.question: Which Helmet setting would you review carefully for an app that loads scripts from a CDN?
Why is Helmet useful but not enough to protect an API?

en.title: Helmet security headers
en.displayQuestion: What is helmet and why should you use it in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check security-header knowledge and defense-in-depth reasoning.
guidance: Define Helmet as middleware that sets security-related HTTP response headers.
Name concrete headers or risk categories it helps address.
Discuss context-aware configuration, especially CSP and HSTS.
Explain that Helmet complements but does not replace authentication, validation, TLS, and other controls.
commonMistakes: Treating Helmet as complete application security.
Not knowing any header it sets.
Breaking frontend assets with careless CSP.
Ignoring app-specific security controls.

vi.title: Helmet và security headers
vi.displayQuestion: helmet là gì và tại sao nên dùng nó trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về security header và defense-in-depth.
guidance: Định nghĩa Helmet là middleware set các HTTP response header liên quan tới bảo mật.
Nêu header cụ thể hoặc nhóm rủi ro mà nó giúp giảm.
Phân tích cấu hình theo context, đặc biệt CSP và HSTS.
Giải thích Helmet chỉ bổ trợ, không thay thế authentication, validation, TLS và các control khác.
commonMistakes: Xem Helmet là bảo mật toàn diện cho app.
Không biết bất kỳ header nào nó set.
Làm hỏng frontend asset vì CSP cấu hình ẩu.
Bỏ qua security control đặc thù của ứng dụng.

ja.title: Helmet と security headers
ja.displayQuestion: helmet とは何で、なぜ Express で使うべきですか。
ja.displayIntent: security header と defense-in-depth の理解を確認する。
guidance: Helmet は security-related HTTP response header を設定する middleware だと定義する。
具体的な header または軽減する risk category を挙げる。
特に CSP と HSTS について context-aware configuration を説明する。
Helmet は authentication、validation、TLS などを置き換えるものではなく補完するものだと説明する。
commonMistakes: Helmet を完全な application security として扱う。
設定される header を 1 つも知らない。
雑な CSP で frontend asset を壊す。
app-specific security control を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Helmet docs
sourceReferences.note: Used for Helmet purpose, default headers, and configuration considerations.

sourceReferences.label: Express security best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express production security guidance and Helmet recommendation.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands the difference between normal middleware continuation and error propagation in Express.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between next(err) and next() in Express?
expectedSignals: Explains next() continues to the next normal matching middleware or route handler.
Explains next(err) tells Express the current request is in an error state and skips remaining non-error middleware.
States errors are handled by error-handling middleware with the signature (err, req, res, next).
Mentions next(err) is used for synchronous callbacks, async failures, validation errors represented as errors, and centralized error response mapping.
Mentions Express 5 automatically calls next(value) for rejected promises or thrown errors from promise-returning middleware/handlers.
Warns that if headers are already sent, the error handler should delegate or avoid trying to send another response.
redFlags: Thinks next(err) sends the error response directly by itself.
Thinks next(err) and next() are interchangeable.
Uses next(err) for normal control flow instead of expected branch handling.
Leaks stack traces or raw internal errors to clients from the error handler.
Calls next(err) after already sending a normal response without considering headersSent.

scoringHints.description: Clearly separates normal continuation from error propagation, explains error middleware signature, async version behavior, and headers-sent safety.
Understands next(err) reaches error handlers but misses Express 5 promises or headers-sent nuance.
Knows next(err) means error but cannot explain what middleware is skipped or how centralized handling works.
Answer would likely cause unhandled errors, hanging requests, or duplicate responses.

followUps.question: What middleware signature must be registered to receive errors passed with next(err)?
What should an error handler do if res.headersSent is already true?
followUps.purpose: Check duplicate response and default handler delegation awareness.

en.title: next(err) versus next()
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between next(err) and next() in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check normal continuation versus error propagation in Express middleware.
guidance: Explain next() as normal continuation.
Explain next(err) as error propagation that skips normal middleware.
Mention the (err, req, res, next) error handler signature.
Discuss async errors, Express 4 versus Express 5, and headers-sent safety.
commonMistakes: Thinking next(err) sends the response itself.
Using next(err) and next() interchangeably.
Forgetting centralized error middleware.
Trying to send another response after headers are already sent.

vi.title: next(err) so với next()
vi.displayQuestion: next(err) và next() khác nhau như thế nào trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra sự khác nhau giữa continuation bình thường và error propagation trong middleware Express.
guidance: Giải thích next() là tiếp tục flow bình thường.
Giải thích next(err) là propagate lỗi và bỏ qua middleware thường.
Nhắc signature error handler là (err, req, res, next).
Nói về async error, khác biệt Express 4 và Express 5, và an toàn khi headers đã gửi.
commonMistakes: Nghĩ next(err) tự gửi response.
Dùng next(err) và next() như nhau.
Quên centralized error middleware.
Cố gửi response thứ hai sau khi headers đã gửi.

ja.title: next(err) と next() の違い
ja.displayQuestion: Express の next(err) と next() は何が違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express middleware における通常継続と error propagation の違いを確認する。
guidance: next() は通常の continuation だと説明する。
next(err) は error propagation で、通常 middleware を skip すると説明する。
error handler signature は (err, req, res, next) だと述べる。
async error、Express 4 と Express 5 の違い、headers sent safety を説明する。
commonMistakes: next(err) が自分で response を送ると考える。
next(err) と next() を同じように使う。
centralized error middleware を忘れる。
headers 送信後にさらに response を送ろうとする。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for next(err), error-handler signature, skipped middleware, and headers-sent behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Writing middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express 5 promise rejection forwarding to next.
